module_info.docstring:
Session management for the gateway.

Handles:
- Session context tracking (where messages come from)
- Session storage (conversations persisted to disk)
- Reset policy evaluation (when to start fresh)
- Dynamic system prompt injection (agent knows its context)

classes.name: SessionSource
classes.docstring:
Describes where a message originated from.

This information is used to:
1. Route responses back to the right place
2. Inject context into the system prompt
3. Track origin for cron job delivery

methods.name: local_cli
methods.docstring: Create a source representing the local CLI.

classes.name: SessionContext
classes.docstring:
Full context for a session, used for dynamic system prompt injection.

The agent receives this information to understand:
- Where messages are coming from
- What platforms are available
- Where it can deliver scheduled task outputs

classes.name: SessionEntry
classes.docstring:
Entry in the session store.

Maps a session key to its current session ID and metadata.

classes.name: SessionStore
classes.docstring:
Manages session storage and retrieval.

Uses SQLite (via SessionDB) for session metadata and message transcripts.
Falls back to legacy JSONL files if SQLite is unavailable.

methods.name: _ensure_loaded
methods.docstring: Load sessions index from disk if not already loaded.

methods.name: _save
methods.docstring: Save sessions index to disk (kept for session key -> ID mapping).

methods.name: _generate_session_key
methods.docstring: Generate a session key from a source.

methods.name: _should_reset
methods.docstring:
Check if a session should be reset based on policy.

Sessions with active background processes are never reset.

methods.name: has_any_sessions
methods.docstring: Check if any sessions have ever been created (across all platforms).

methods.name: get_or_create_session
methods.docstring:
Get an existing session or create a new one.

Evaluates reset policy to determine if the existing session is stale.
Creates a session record in SQLite when a new session starts.

methods.name: update_session
methods.docstring: Update a session's metadata after an interaction.

methods.name: reset_session
methods.docstring: Force reset a session, creating a new session ID.

methods.name: list_sessions
methods.docstring: List all sessions, optionally filtered by activity.

methods.name: get_transcript_path
methods.docstring: Get the path to a session's legacy transcript file.

methods.name: append_to_transcript
methods.docstring: Append a message to a session's transcript (SQLite + legacy JSONL).

methods.name: rewrite_transcript
methods.docstring:
Replace the entire transcript for a session with new messages.

Used by /retry, /undo, and /compress to persist modified conversation history.
Rewrites both SQLite and legacy JSONL storage.

methods.name: load_transcript
methods.docstring: Load all messages from a session's transcript.

functions.name: build_session_context_prompt
functions.docstring:
Build the dynamic system prompt section that tells the agent about its context.

This is injected into the system prompt so the agent knows:
- Where messages are coming from
- What platforms are connected
- Where it can deliver scheduled task outputs

functions.name: build_session_context
functions.docstring:
Build a full session context from a source and config.

This is used to inject context into the agent's system prompt.
